reason: Both reviews confirm a well-structured world. Playtest: PASS with no P0/P1, average 3.89, minimum 3. Enrichment: 7 suggestions, all enrichments (none blocking). All pass criteria met.
p2Defects: P2-1: Verify fun_prop cannot close L01/L02/L04/L07/L08 injectivity goals; add to DisabledTactic if it can
P2-2: congrArg mentioned in L05 conclusion and Branch but no NewTheorem declaration; learner won't see it in theorem panel
P2-3: L01 introduction claims omega cannot read lambda form directly — verify whether intro a b hab; omega works; if so, reframe have step as good practice rather than necessity
enrichmentSuggestionsToImplement: E1: Add counterexample level for 'Injective (g ∘ f) does NOT imply Injective g' — strongest suggestion, currently claimed but unproved in L05 intro
E2: Make congrArg a required proof move (not just a Branch/footnote in L05) — described as recurring but never required
E4: Acknowledge injection tactic's existence in L01 conclusion — low effort, prevents confusion, teaches a real Lean feature
E5: Name the 'peeling' strategy in L04 conclusion — low effort, aids cross-world vocabulary
E3: Add concrete composition instantiation level after L04 — valuable grounding but boss partially fills this role
E7: Add one concrete motivation sentence to world introduction — good but current intro is already strong
E6: Mention left-cancellation characterization in L08 conclusion — mathematically significant but may exceed scope
playtestFixesRequired: Verify fun_prop cannot close injectivity goals (P2-1)
Verify omega behavior after bare intro in L01 (P2-3)
Decide on congrArg formalization: add NewTheorem or remove Branch (P2-2)
notes: World passes all quantitative thresholds. The enrichment reviewer's top suggestion (counterexample level for the false converse) is the single strongest improvement opportunity — L05 lines 27-29 state a mathematical fact without proving it, which is a missed teaching moment in a proof game. This is a genuine enrichment rather than a defect: the claim is in an introductory note, not a learning objective, and the playtest accounts for deferred testing in W12 pset. The three P2 items are verification tasks that may require minor text edits but no structural changes.
